ruleset.name: Pathfinder 2nd Edition
ruleset.license: Original mechanical references; Pathfinder 2e ruleset content under Paizo's ORC license. Spell, class, and monster names belong to Paizo Publishing.
ruleset.summary: Pathfinder 2nd Edition is a tightly mathematical d20 fantasy system. A check is 1d20 + ability modifier + proficiency bonus + item bonus vs. a Difficulty Class, with four degrees of success: critical success at +10 over the DC, success at meeting it, failure under it, critical failure at -10 below. The three-action economy and Multiple Attack Penalty give every turn meaningful tactical choice.

Trivial.description: Untrained character can manage with average stats.

Low.description: Trained at character's level expects to succeed.

Moderate.description: The standard challenge for an at-level expert.

High.description: Pushes a master character; a trained one will need luck.

Severe.description: A serious test even for a Master at level.

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.description: Raw physical force. Adds to Athletics, melee attack rolls with non-finesse weapons, and melee damage.

attributes.name: Dexterity
attributes.description: Agility, balance, fine motor control. Adds to AC (capped by armor's Dex cap), Reflex saves, ranged attacks, and finesse melee attacks.

attributes.name: Constitution
attributes.description: Health and endurance. Adds to Hit Points each level and to Fortitude saves.

attributes.name: Intelligence
attributes.description: Memory, reasoning, education. Grants extra trained skills and Lores at character creation; adds to skills like Arcana, Crafting, Society.

attributes.name: Wisdom
attributes.description: Awareness, intuition, common sense. Adds to Perception, Will saves, and skills like Medicine, Nature, Religion, Survival.

attributes.name: Charisma
attributes.description: Presence, persuasion, force of personality. Adds to social skills (Diplomacy, Deception, Intimidation, Performance, Society) and to spell DCs for Charisma-based casters.

saves.name: Fortitude
saves.description: Resists poison, disease, and physical effects. Constitution + proficiency.

saves.name: Reflex
saves.description: Dodges traps, area effects, and quick hazards. Dexterity + proficiency.

saves.name: Will
saves.description: Resists mind-affecting effects, fear, and possession. Wisdom + proficiency.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: Tumbling, balancing, escaping grabs. Trained-only actions: Maneuver in Flight.

skills.name: Arcana
skills.description: Knowledge of arcane magic and creatures. Trained-only: Identify Magic, Learn a Spell, Decipher Writing of arcane origin.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.description: Climbing, jumping, swimming, forceful maneuvers (Grapple, Trip, Shove, Disarm). High Jump and Long Jump are signature actions.

skills.name: Crafting
skills.description: Making and repairing items. Trained-only: Craft, Repair, Identify Alchemy.

skills.name: Deception
skills.description: Lying, disguise, feinting in combat. Trained-only: Impersonate.

skills.name: Diplomacy
skills.description: Making requests, gathering information, swaying attitudes. Always available untrained.

skills.name: Intimidation
skills.description: Coercing or demoralizing. Demoralize is a frequent combat action.

skills.name: Lore
skills.description: A specific narrow expertise the player chose at character creation (Sailing Lore, Goblin Lore, Absalom Lore, Carpentry Lore, etc.). Each Lore is a separate trained skill; the GM allows it for narrow Recall Knowledge questions.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.description: First aid, treating poison/disease. Trained-only: Treat Disease, Treat Poison, Treat Wounds.

skills.name: Nature
skills.description: Knowledge of plants, animals, weather. Trained-only: Command an Animal, Identify Magic of primal origin.

skills.name: Occultism
skills.description: Knowledge of the occult — esoteric philosophy, ghosts, aberrations. Trained-only: Identify Magic of occult origin.

skills.name: Performance
skills.description: Music, oratory, dance, acting. Trained-only: Perform (extended).

skills.name: Religion
skills.description: Knowledge of deities, dogmas, the planes. Trained-only: Identify Magic of divine origin.

skills.name: Society
skills.description: History, etiquette, languages, current affairs. Trained-only: Decipher Writing, Subsist (in civilization).

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Hiding and sneaking. Used to oppose Perception when avoiding detection.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: Wilderness skills: tracking, foraging, weathering. Trained-only: Cover Tracks, Track.

skills.name: Thievery
skills.description: Picking locks, picking pockets, disabling traps. Trained-only: Disable Device, Pick a Lock.

derivedStats.name: Hit Points
derivedStats.formula: (class HP per level + CON_mod) per character level + ancestry HP at level 1

derivedStats.name: Armor Class
derivedStats.formula: 10 + DEX_mod (capped by armor) + armor proficiency bonus + armor item bonus

derivedStats.name: Class DC
derivedStats.formula: 10 + class proficiency bonus + key ability modifier

derivedStats.name: Spell DC
derivedStats.formula: 10 + spell proficiency bonus + casting ability modifier

derivedStats.name: Perception
derivedStats.formula: WIS_mod + perception proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Fortitude Save
derivedStats.formula: CON_mod + fortitude proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Reflex Save
derivedStats.formula: DEX_mod + reflex proficiency bonus + item bonus

derivedStats.name: Will Save
derivedStats.formula: WIS_mod + will proficiency bonus + item bonus

values.label: Off-Guard
values.description: -2 circumstance penalty to AC. Renamed from 'flat-footed' in the Remaster. Not graduated — either you are off-guard or you aren't. Most attackers gain off-guard against a target they've successfully feinted; many other effects (grabbing, restraining, stunning) also impose it.
values.trigger: Being flanked, prone, grabbed, restrained, climbing, hidden from your attacker, successfully feinted, or affected by an ability or maneuver that explicitly imposes off-guard.

values.label: Frightened
values.description: Graduated 1-4. Take a status penalty equal to the current value on ALL checks and DCs (attack rolls, saves, skill checks, AC, class DC, spell DC, etc.). At the end of each of your turns the value decreases by 1 unless an effect specifies otherwise. Frightened 4 is the maximum; some effects cap a creature at lower values (e.g. successful save = frightened 1).
values.trigger: Failing a Demoralize check, failing a save against a fear effect, witnessing a particularly grim event, or any ability that explicitly inflicts the frightened condition.

values.label: Sickened
values.description: Graduated 1-4. Take a status penalty equal to the current value on all checks and DCs, AND cannot willingly ingest anything (food, potion, elixir). You may spend 1 action retching to attempt a Fortitude save: success reduces sickened by 1, critical success reduces by 2; failure does nothing; critical failure increases by 1. Allies may use Battle Medicine or comparable abilities to grant a bonus to the save.
values.trigger: Failing a save against a disgust, poison, or disease effect; taking an action that revolts the character; or any ability that explicitly inflicts the sickened condition.

values.label: Stunned
values.description: Numeric value OR a duration. When numeric (e.g. stunned 2), at the start of your turn lose actions equal to the value, then reduce the stunned value by the actions lost; if the value exceeds your remaining actions, the overflow carries into the next turn. When durational (e.g. stunned for 1 minute), you lose ALL actions on your turn until the duration ends. Stunned doesn't reduce reactions you've already gained.
values.trigger: Failing a save against a stunning effect, certain critical hits, monk Stunning Strike, or any ability that explicitly inflicts the stunned condition.

values.label: Slowed
values.description: Lose actions equal to the value at the start of each turn (does not stack with Stunned for the lost-action calculation; take the worse).

values.label: Quickened
values.description: Gain 1 extra action at the start of each turn, usable only for the specified purpose granted by the source.

values.label: Prone
values.description: You're lying on the ground. You take a -2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and are off-guard. You can Crawl (1 action, 5 ft) or Stand (1 action) to leave prone. Ranged attackers against you may suffer additional penalties depending on cover and range. You can Take Cover while prone to gain greater cover against ranged attacks.
values.trigger: Failing a Trip (Athletics) check against you, certain area effects and spells that knock targets prone, voluntarily Dropping Prone (1 action), or any ability that explicitly inflicts the prone condition.

values.label: Grabbed
values.description: Off-guard and immobilized; you can Escape (Acrobatics or Athletics, or attempt a basic check) as an action.

values.label: Restrained
values.description: Off-guard and immobilized; cannot use manipulate actions other than Escape or to break free.

values.label: Wounded
values.description: Graduated 1-3+. Whenever you lose the dying condition, your wounded value increases by 1 (or by 2 if you were dying 4 or higher). The next time you'd become dying, add your wounded value to the dying value — meaning subsequent knockouts come faster. Wounded only fully removes when you've been at full HP for 10 minutes of rest (or via Treat Wounds at appropriate proficiency that explicitly removes wounded).
values.trigger: Losing the dying condition by recovering to positive HP; the value increases each time you go from dying-N back to alive.

values.label: Dying
values.description: Graduated 1-3 (with 4 = death). You're unconscious and progressing toward death. Becoming dying for the first time starts you at dying 1 (or dying 2 if dropped by a critical hit, or dying 1 + wounded value if already wounded). At the start of each of your turns make a recovery flat check: DC 10 + current dying. Success reduces dying by 1, critical success by 2; failure increases by 1, critical failure by 2. Reaching dying 4 means you die. Taking damage while dying increases the value by 1 (by 2 if critical). Regaining at least 1 HP removes dying entirely (but inflicts wounded).
values.trigger: Being reduced to 0 HP from positive HP — automatically dying 1 (or dying 2 on a critical hit). Add your current wounded value to the dying value when you enter dying.

values.label: Doomed
values.description: Graduated 1-3+. Your effective maximum dying value (normally 4) is reduced by your doomed value. So doomed 1 means dying 3 = death; doomed 2 means dying 2 = death; doomed 3 means dying 1 = death; doomed 4 means going dying at all = death. Doomed reduces by 1 each time you take a full night's rest (typically 8 hours). Some powerful effects bypass normal restoration — only high-rank divine intervention can remove doomed early.
values.trigger: Failing a save against a death effect, certain demons' aura, or any ability that explicitly inflicts the doomed condition. Doomed does NOT automatically come from being knocked out — only from specified sources.

values.label: Confused
values.description: You attack any creature in reach (or use cantrip on a random adjacent target if no melee). You're off-guard.

values.label: Fascinated
values.description: -2 status to Perception and skill checks; cannot Concentrate or use actions with concentrate trait other than Seek; ends if hostile action is taken against you.

values.label: Drained
values.description: Graduated 1-4. Status penalty equal to the value applies to all Constitution-based checks (including Fortitude saves). Your maximum HP is reduced by your level × drained value, and you lose current HP equal to your level × the AMOUNT the drained value increased by when first applied (so going from drained 0 to drained 2 deals level × 2 immediate damage). The value decreases by 1 every full night's rest. Restoring drained doesn't restore the lost HP — those need to be healed normally.
values.trigger: Failing a save against a negative-energy effect, certain undead's life-drain attacks, severe disease progression, vampire bite, or any ability that explicitly inflicts the drained condition.

values.label: Enfeebled
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Strength-based rolls and damage.

values.label: Stupefied
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Intelligence/Wisdom/Charisma checks and to spell DCs and spell attack rolls.

values.label: Clumsy
values.description: Status penalty equal to value to Dexterity-based rolls and to AC.

values.label: Persistent Damage
values.description: Damage of a specific type (e.g. persistent fire 1d6, persistent bleed 2d4) applied at the END of each of your turns. After the damage, attempt a flat check DC 15 (or DC 10 if you or an ally took an action to provide assistance appropriate to the damage type — pouring water on fire, bandaging bleed, scraping off acid) to end the condition. Each damage type has its own track and assistance method; multiple persistent damages run in parallel. Resistances and weaknesses apply normally.
values.trigger: Critical hits with weapons that deal bleed/poison/acid/fire, alchemical splash items, certain spells (Flaming Sphere, Acid Arrow), and creature special attacks. Persistent damage is suffixed by its type (persistent bleed, persistent fire, etc.).

values.label: Unconscious
values.description: Off-guard; -4 status to AC, Perception, and Reflex; cannot act. Wakes if HP restored or shaken (1 action) and DC 5 Perception.

conditions.name: Off-Guard
conditions.description: -2 circumstance penalty to AC. Renamed from 'flat-footed' in the Remaster. Triggered by being flanked, prone, grabbed, restrained, climbing, hidden from your attacker, successfully feinted, or affected by an ability that explicitly imposes off-guard.

conditions.name: Frightened
conditions.description: Status penalty equal to value to all checks and DCs. Decreases by 1 at end of each turn.

conditions.name: Sickened
conditions.description: Status penalty equal to value to all checks and DCs. Spend 1 action and Fortitude save to reduce.

conditions.name: Prone
conditions.description: -2 circumstance to attacks; off-guard. Standing up is a single action.

conditions.name: Drained
conditions.description: Graduated 1-4 status penalty to all Constitution-based checks (including Fortitude saves) and to HP maximum. Initial application also deals immediate level × increase damage. Decreases by 1 per full night's rest; lost HP must be healed normally.

conditions.name: Enfeebled
conditions.description: Status penalty equal to value to Strength-based rolls and damage.

conditions.name: Stupefied
conditions.description: Graduated 1-4 status penalty to all Intelligence-, Wisdom-, and Charisma-based checks, spell DCs, and spell attack rolls. When attempting to Cast a Spell, first attempt a flat check DC 5 + stupefied value; failure makes the spell fizzle and lose its slot/cost. Hits casters particularly hard.

conditions.name: Clumsy
conditions.description: Status penalty to Dexterity-based rolls and to AC.

conditions.name: Fascinated
conditions.description: -2 status penalty to Perception and to all skill checks. You cannot use actions with the concentrate trait other than Seek (no Casting Spells, no Sustain). The condition ends automatically if any creature uses hostile action against you or any ally you can perceive, and you can attempt a Will save against the source's DC at the end of each of your turns.

lorebook.name: Pathfinder 2e Rules Reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered rules reference for the GM agent running a Pathfinder Second Edition (Remaster) game. Each entry surfaces only when its keys appear in recent narration or player input, so the GM gets the right rule at the right moment without inflating every prompt.

entries.name: Three Action Economy
entries.content: Each turn a creature gets 3 actions, 1 reaction, and any number of free actions. Most things cost 1 action (Strike, Stride, Demoralize); some cost 2 (Sustain a 2-action spell, two-action activities); a few cost 3 (full-round spells, Confident Finisher). Reactions trigger on specified events (Attack of Opportunity, Shield Block) outside your turn. Multiple Attack Penalty (MAP): your first Strike per turn is at +0, second at -5 (or -4 with an agile weapon), third at -10 (or -8 agile). MAP resets at the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Degrees of Success
entries.content: Every d20 check resolves on four bands: critical success when the result beats the DC by 10 or more (or a natural 20 bumps a success up one band); success when the result meets or exceeds the DC; failure when the result is below the DC; critical failure when the result is 10 or more under the DC (or a natural 1 bumps a failure down one band). Natural 20 and natural 1 are SHIFTS, not autocrits — a nat 20 on a roll that would still miss by 10 only upgrades miss to hit. Spells, attacks, and skill actions all read their effects off this four-band ladder.

entries.name: Proficiency Tiers (UTEML)
entries.content: Proficiency in skills, saves, weapons, and class DC is one of five tiers: Untrained (+0, no level), Trained (+2 + Level), Expert (+4 + Level), Master (+6 + Level), Legendary (+8 + Level). Level adds to the bonus at every trained-or-higher tier, which is why PF2e math stays tight as parties scale. Many skill actions are Trained-only (Treat Wounds, Identify Magic, Disable Device). Class features and skill increases raise tiers at specific levels.

entries.name: Hero Points
entries.content: Each PC starts the session with 1 Hero Point and gains 1 per hour of significant play (max 3 banked). Spend 1 Hero Point to reroll a check you just made and take the better result, OR spend ALL Hero Points to avoid death when reduced to dying with no Stabilize available. Hero Points reset to 1 at the start of each session — banking is not cross-session. Encourage spending; the system assumes PCs use them.

entries.name: Dying and Wounded
entries.content: At 0 HP from positive, you fall unconscious and gain dying 1 (dying 2 on a critical hit; add your current wounded value to the starting dying value). At the start of each of your turns make a recovery flat check: DC 10 + current dying. Success reduces dying by 1, critical success by 2; failure increases by 1, critical failure by 2. Reaching dying 4 means you die. Regaining at least 1 HP removes dying entirely but increases wounded by 1 (or 2 if you were dying 4+). Wounded only clears after 10 minutes at full HP or with Treat Wounds that explicitly removes it. Doomed reduces the dying-4 death threshold (doomed 1 = die at dying 3, etc.); decreases by 1 per full night's rest.

entries.name: Common Conditions
entries.content: Off-Guard: -2 circumstance penalty to AC; renamed from flat-footed in the Remaster. Frightened/Sickened/Clumsy/Enfeebled/Stupefied/Drained: graduated 1-4, apply a status penalty equal to the value to relevant rolls; frightened decreases by 1 each turn-end automatically. Stunned: lose actions equal to the value at the start of your turn. Slowed: lose actions equal to the value at the start of each turn (does not stack with Stunned — take the worse). Prone: -2 circumstance to attacks; off-guard. Persistent Damage (bleed/fire/acid/etc.): take the listed damage at the END of each of your turns, then flat-check DC 15 (DC 10 with assistance) to end.

entries.name: Saves and DCs
entries.content: Three saves: Fortitude (CON + prof), Reflex (DEX + prof), Will (WIS + prof). Spell DC = 10 + spell proficiency bonus + casting attribute mod. Class DC = 10 + class proficiency bonus + key ability mod. Set encounter DCs from the Difficulty table: Trivial 10, Low 15, Moderate 20, High 25, Severe 30, Extreme 40 — adjust by level. Recall Knowledge uses the Lore/Society/Arcana/etc. DC from the same table tied to the subject's level.

entries.name: Spellcasting and Slots
entries.content: Prepared casters fill spell slots at a daily preparation; spontaneous casters cast known spells from slots of the appropriate rank (PF2e uses 'rank' instead of 'level' for spells post-Remaster). Cantrips are at-will and auto-heighten to half your level rounded up. Focus spells use Focus Points (refocus during a 10-minute rest to regain 1 point per focus spell trained). Heightening a spell at a higher rank often adds dice or extra targets — read the spell's heightened entry. Sustained spells require a sustain action (1 action) on each subsequent turn to remain in effect.

entries.name: Attribute: Strength (STR)
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition attribute. Strength (STR) — Raw physical force. Adds to Athletics, melee attack rolls with non-finesse weapons, and melee damage.

entries.name: Attribute: Dexterity (DEX)
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition attribute. Dexterity (DEX) — Agility, balance, fine motor control. Adds to AC (capped by armor's Dex cap), Reflex saves, ranged attacks, and finesse melee attacks.

entries.name: Attribute: Constitution (CON)
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition attribute. Constitution (CON) — Health and endurance. Adds to Hit Points each level and to Fortitude saves.

entries.name: Attribute: Intelligence (INT)
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition attribute. Intelligence (INT) — Memory, reasoning, education. Grants extra trained skills and Lores at character creation; adds to skills like Arcana, Crafting, Society.

entries.name: Attribute: Wisdom (WIS)
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition attribute. Wisdom (WIS) — Awareness, intuition, common sense. Adds to Perception, Will saves, and skills like Medicine, Nature, Religion, Survival.

entries.name: Attribute: Charisma (CHA)
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition attribute. Charisma (CHA) — Presence, persuasion, force of personality. Adds to social skills (Diplomacy, Deception, Intimidation, Performance, Society) and to spell DCs for Charisma-based casters.

entries.name: Skill: Acrobatics
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Acrobatics. Tumbling, balancing, escaping grabs. Trained-only actions: Maneuver in Flight.

entries.name: Skill: Arcana
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Arcana. Knowledge of arcane magic and creatures. Trained-only: Identify Magic, Learn a Spell, Decipher Writing of arcane origin.

entries.name: Skill: Athletics
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Athletics. Climbing, jumping, swimming, forceful maneuvers (Grapple, Trip, Shove, Disarm). High Jump and Long Jump are signature actions.

entries.name: Skill: Crafting
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Crafting. Making and repairing items. Trained-only: Craft, Repair, Identify Alchemy.

entries.name: Skill: Deception
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Deception. Lying, disguise, feinting in combat. Trained-only: Impersonate.

entries.name: Skill: Diplomacy
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Diplomacy. Making requests, gathering information, swaying attitudes. Always available untrained.

entries.name: Skill: Intimidation
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Intimidation. Coercing or demoralizing. Demoralize is a frequent combat action.

entries.name: Skill: Lore
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Lore. A specific narrow expertise the player chose at character creation (Sailing Lore, Goblin Lore, Absalom Lore, Carpentry Lore, etc.). Each Lore is a separate trained skill; the GM allows it for narrow Recall Knowledge questions.

entries.name: Skill: Medicine
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Medicine. First aid, treating poison/disease. Trained-only: Treat Disease, Treat Poison, Treat Wounds.

entries.name: Skill: Nature
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Nature. Knowledge of plants, animals, weather. Trained-only: Command an Animal, Identify Magic of primal origin.

entries.name: Skill: Occultism
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Occultism. Knowledge of the occult — esoteric philosophy, ghosts, aberrations. Trained-only: Identify Magic of occult origin.

entries.name: Skill: Performance
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Performance. Music, oratory, dance, acting. Trained-only: Perform (extended).

entries.name: Skill: Religion
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Religion. Knowledge of deities, dogmas, the planes. Trained-only: Identify Magic of divine origin.

entries.name: Skill: Society
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Society. History, etiquette, languages, current affairs. Trained-only: Decipher Writing, Subsist (in civilization).

entries.name: Skill: Stealth
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Stealth. Hiding and sneaking. Used to oppose Perception when avoiding detection.

entries.name: Skill: Survival
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Survival. Wilderness skills: tracking, foraging, weathering. Trained-only: Cover Tracks, Track.

entries.name: Skill: Thievery
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition skill. Thievery. Picking locks, picking pockets, disabling traps. Trained-only: Disable Device, Pick a Lock.

entries.name: Condition: Off-Guard
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Off-Guard — -2 circumstance penalty to AC. Renamed from 'flat-footed' in the Remaster. Triggered by being flanked, prone, grabbed, restrained, climbing, hidden from your attacker, successfully feinted, or affected by an ability that explicitly imposes off-guard.

entries.name: Condition: Frightened
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Frightened — Status penalty equal to value to all checks and DCs. Decreases by 1 at end of each turn.

entries.name: Condition: Sickened
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Sickened — Status penalty equal to value to all checks and DCs. Spend 1 action and Fortitude save to reduce.

entries.name: Condition: Prone
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Prone — -2 circumstance to attacks; off-guard. Standing up is a single action.

entries.name: Condition: Drained
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Drained — Graduated 1-4 status penalty to all Constitution-based checks (including Fortitude saves) and to HP maximum. Initial application also deals immediate level × increase damage. Decreases by 1 per full night's rest; lost HP must be healed normally.

entries.name: Condition: Enfeebled
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Enfeebled — Status penalty equal to value to Strength-based rolls and damage.

entries.name: Condition: Stupefied
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Stupefied — Graduated 1-4 status penalty to all Intelligence-, Wisdom-, and Charisma-based checks, spell DCs, and spell attack rolls. When attempting to Cast a Spell, first attempt a flat check DC 5 + stupefied value; failure makes the spell fizzle and lose its slot/cost. Hits casters particularly hard.

entries.name: Condition: Clumsy
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Clumsy — Status penalty to Dexterity-based rolls and to AC.

entries.name: Condition: Fascinated
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition condition. Fascinated — -2 status penalty to Perception and to all skill checks. You cannot use actions with the concentrate trait other than Seek (no Casting Spells, no Sustain). The condition ends automatically if any creature uses hostile action against you or any ally you can perceive, and you can attempt a Will save against the source's DC at the end of each of your turns.

entries.name: Derived stat: Hit Points
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Hit Points — formula: (class HP per level + CON_mod) per character level + ancestry HP at level 1

entries.name: Derived stat: Armor Class
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Armor Class — formula: 10 + DEX_mod (capped by armor) + armor proficiency bonus + armor item bonus

entries.name: Derived stat: Class DC
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Class DC — formula: 10 + class proficiency bonus + key ability modifier

entries.name: Derived stat: Spell DC
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Spell DC — formula: 10 + spell proficiency bonus + casting ability modifier

entries.name: Derived stat: Perception

entries.name: Derived stat: Fortitude Save

entries.name: Derived stat: Reflex Save
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Reflex Save — formula: DEX_mod + reflex proficiency bonus + item bonus

entries.name: Derived stat: Will Save
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Will Save — formula: WIS_mod + will proficiency bonus + item bonus

entries.name: Derived stat: Speed
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Speed — formula: Ancestry base speed (typically 25 ft) + bonuses

entries.name: Derived stat: Level
entries.content: Pathfinder 2nd Edition derived stat. Level — formula: Character level, 1-20.

entries.name: Difficulty ladder reference
entries.content:
Pathfinder 2nd Edition difficulty ladder. Threshold values needed for success:
- Trivial (threshold 10) — Untrained character can manage with average stats.
- Low (threshold 15) — Trained at character's level expects to succeed.
- Moderate (threshold 20) — The standard challenge for an at-level expert.
- High (threshold 25) — Pushes a master character; a trained one will need luck.
- Severe (threshold 30) — A serious test even for a Master at level.
- Extreme (threshold 40) — Legendary territory. Stack every bonus.

customTools.name: pathfinder2e_reference
customTools.description: Returns canonical Pathfinder 2nd Edition reference: dice, resolution, difficulty ladder, attributes, skills. Call when the GM needs authoritative ruleset detail mid-narration.
customTools.staticResult:
# Pathfinder 2nd Edition — canonical reference

Pathfinder 2nd Edition is a tightly mathematical d20 fantasy system. A check is 1d20 + ability modifier + proficiency bonus + item bonus vs. a Difficulty Class, with four degrees of success: critical success at +10 over the DC, success at meeting it, failure under it, critical failure at -10 below. The three-action economy and Multiple Attack Penalty give every turn meaningful tactical choice.

## Dice
- Type: `d20`
- Notation: `1d20+MOD`

## Resolution
- Mode: `single-roll`

## Difficulty ladder
- **Trivial** (threshold 10) — Untrained character can manage with average stats.
- **Low** (threshold 15) — Trained at character's level expects to succeed.
- **Moderate** (threshold 20) — The standard challenge for an at-level expert.
- **High** (threshold 25) — Pushes a master character; a trained one will need luck.
- **Severe** (threshold 30) — A serious test even for a Master at level.
- **Extreme** (threshold 40) — Legendary territory. Stack every bonus.

## Attributes
- **Strength** (STR) — Raw physical force. Adds to Athletics, melee attack rolls with non-finesse weapons, and melee damage.
- **Dexterity** (DEX) — Agility, balance, fine motor control. Adds to AC (capped by armor's Dex cap), Reflex saves, ranged attacks, and finesse melee attacks.
- **Constitution** (CON) — Health and endurance. Adds to Hit Points each level and to Fortitude saves.
- **Intelligence** (INT) — Memory, reasoning, education. Grants extra trained skills and Lores at character creation; adds to skills like Arcana, Crafting, Society.
- **Wisdom** (WIS) — Awareness, intuition, common sense. Adds to Perception, Will saves, and skills like Medicine, Nature, Religion, Survival.
- **Charisma** (CHA) — Presence, persuasion, force of personality. Adds to social skills (Diplomacy, Deception, Intimidation, Performance, Society) and to spell DCs for Charisma-based casters.

## Skills
- **Acrobatics** — Tumbling, balancing, escaping grabs. Trained-only actions: Maneuver in Flight.
- **Arcana** — Knowledge of arcane magic and creatures. Trained-only: Identify Magic, Learn a Spell, Decipher Writing of arcane origin.
- **Athletics** — Climbing, jumping, swimming, forceful maneuvers (Grapple, Trip, Shove, Disarm). High Jump and Long Jump are signature actions.
- **Crafting** — Making and repairing items. Trained-only: Craft, Repair, Identify Alchemy.
- **Deception** — Lying, disguise, feinting in combat. Trained-only: Impersonate.
- **Diplomacy** — Making requests, gathering information, swaying attitudes. Always available untrained.
- **Intimidation** — Coercing or demoralizing. Demoralize is a frequent combat action.
- **Lore** — A specific narrow expertise the player chose at character creation (Sailing Lore, Goblin Lore, Absalom Lore, Carpentry Lore, etc.). Each Lore is a separate trained skill; the GM allows it for narrow Recall Knowledge questions.
- **Medicine** — First aid, treating poison/disease. Trained-only: Treat Disease, Treat Poison, Treat Wounds.
- **Nature** — Knowledge of plants, animals, weather. Trained-only: Command an Animal, Identify Magic of primal origin.
- **Occultism** — Knowledge of the occult — esoteric philosophy, ghosts, aberrations. Trained-only: Identify Magic of occult origin.
- **Performance** — Music, oratory, dance, acting. Trained-only: Perform (extended).
- **Religion** — Knowledge of deities, dogmas, the planes. Trained-only: Identify Magic of divine origin.
- **Society** — History, etiquette, languages, current affairs. Trained-only: Decipher Writing, Subsist (in civilization).
- **Stealth** — Hiding and sneaking. Used to oppose Perception when avoiding detection.
- **Survival** — Wilderness skills: tracking, foraging, weathering. Trained-only: Cover Tracks, Track.
- **Thievery** — Picking locks, picking pockets, disabling traps. Trained-only: Disable Device, Pick a Lock.

## Commitment
- Model: `invested`
